validation_rules: compose existing public formal-math component organs before emitting kernel claims
reject forbidden forward-packet proof, oracle, repair, source-body, and base-index fields
reject oracle comparator rows counted as forward success
reject provider or NIM rows that claim proof authority
reject CP2 candidates that carry proof bodies, raw tactic scripts, provider bodies, or oracle templates
reject Evolve candidates outside bounded policy artifacts
require exported runtime bundles to carry source_module_manifest.json rows for copied verifier-lab kernel and component organ source bodies
verify copied source module targets exist and match source_module_manifest sha256 digests
require separated claim buckets for verifier, oracle, provider, contract, retrieval, CP2, and Evolve outcomes
emit secret-exclusion scan and structured public runtime receipts with proof/provider/oracle/private-source/stdout/stderr bodies outside receipt bodies
receipt_expectations: receipt must cite component statuses, Lean/Lake return code, target-shape route count, verifier trace attempt count, source module import status, source-open body import floor, negative-case coverage, claim separation, authority counters, receipt transparency contract, secret-exclusion scan, public runtime refs, real-runtime receipt status, and anti-claim

receipt_transparency_contract.rule: receipts are public runtime evidence; proof/provider/oracle/private-source/stdout/stderr payload bodies stay outside receipt bodies without replacing source-available substrate

allowed_public_inputs: Lean/Lake return codes and stdout/stderr line counts

anti_claim: This standard governs public verifier-lab composition receipts only; it does not authorize private proof-body import, oracle success as forward proof, provider proof authority, CP2 proof bodies, arbitrary Evolve mutation, benchmark solve-rate claims, release, publication, or private-data equivalence.

standard_payload.ontology: A source-open verifier-governed lab kernel that composes forward problems, oracle sidecars, verifier attempts, provider hypotheses, residual diagnoses, CP2 typed action candidates, bounded Evolve candidates, and structured public runtime receipts whose proof/provider/oracle/private-source/stdout/stderr payload bodies stay outside receipt bodies.
standard_payload.teleology: Make Microcosm execute the macro formal-math epistemics pattern without importing private proof bodies or upgrading advisory outputs into proof authority.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: Moving private Lean proof bodies wholesale or treating provider/oracle output as theorem proof.
failure_modes: forward packet leaks proof or oracle body
oracle comparator is counted as forward success
CP2 candidate carries proof body or raw tactic script
Evolve candidate mutates unbounded source artifact
positive metric lacks anti-claim and receipt ref

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; accepted organ registry and receipt anchors bound the source row but do not grant release, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness authority.

doctrine_population_status.authority_boundary: public verifier-lab fixture, exported-bundle, source-module manifest, capsule, mechanism, and receipt refs only; no private proof-body import, theorem-prover proof authority, oracle-forward promotion, provider proof authority, benchmark solve-rate claim, release, source mutation, or private-data equivalence

omission_receipt.reason: public runtime refs and receipts carry the verifier-lab substrate while proof, provider, oracle-answer, private-source, and stdout/stderr payload bodies stay outside receipt bodies

anti_claim_contract.rule: verifier lab material must state that provider and oracle outputs are not proof authority

source_coupling_contract.rule: macro source refs stay path-level metadata, public runtime refs stay public-root relative, and copied verifier-lab source body rows must bind source_ref, target_ref, sha256, body_copied=true, body_in_receipt=false, and exact_copy relation

author.purpose: create a valid Leak-proof verifier lab kernel instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Leak-proof verifier lab kernel without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_verifier_lab_kernel and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections
